title: Result - San-marino
content:
Welcome to San Marino, a picturesque microstate in north-central Italy, spanning just 61 square kilometres. Centred around Mount Titano, it boasts breathtaking views of the Adriatic Sea and Apennines. Recognized as a UNESCO World Heritage Site, San Marino’s medieval old town enchants with narrow streets, historic monuments like the Three Towers and cultural treasures like the Basilica of Saint Marinus.
Enjoy outdoor adventures, from hiking and cycling trails to free climbing and horseback riding. Savor local foods and wines while attending vibrant cultural events. With a diverse economy driven by manufacturing, trade and tourism, San Marino offers a unique blend of history and modern charm.

## Unique food tradition
Torta Tre Monti (three mountain cake): a circular multi-layered cake with 5 thin wafers filled with layers of smooth chocolate, hazelnut and cream.
San Marino has a strong economy based on tourism, finance and agriculture.

### Local legend or myth
Saint Marinus: a stone cutter from Dalmatia fled persecution in the third century, founding the independent republic of San Marino on Mount Titano, which still exists today.

### Language of the Land
Italian is the official language, however a local dialect is also spoken and is considered the second language of San Marino.
square kilometres is the area of San Marino, making it one of the smallest countries in the world.
